title: Multi-Agent Delegation, Context Minimization, Merge, and Failure Containment
description: A coordinator sends full user context and credentials to many agents, gives them shared write tools, accepts unvalidated summaries, and amplifies one poisoned result across the system. Learners cumulatively produce typed contained delegation graph through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use delegation only when specialized context, independent work, isolation, or ownership beats a simpler function, workflow, tool, or single agent.
Define delegated goal, inputs, outputs, non-goals, authority, tools, budget, deadline, evidence, stop, and escalation in a typed contract.
Send only task-required, tenant-authorized, provenance-labeled context and never inherit parent secrets or tools implicitly.
